title: SynthEd: From Synthetic Data to Simulated Learners
description:
SynthEd is an agent-based simulation environment for open and distance learning (ODL) research. By combining persona-driven agent modeling with 10 established theoretical frameworks, it generates behaviorally grounded and temporally coherent learning trajectories. Each synthetic student's engagement, dropout risk, GPA, and social interactions emerge from evolving motivations, experiences, and life contexts.

Simulation Engine

  • 10 theory modules (Tinto, Bean & Metzner, Kember, SDT, Garrison CoI, Moore, Rovai, Bäulke, Epstein & Axtell, Gonzalez) with 4-phase protocol dispatch and auto-discovery
  • Continuous persona spectrum: employment intensity, family responsibility, internet reliability as [0,1] floats with Beta distributions
  • Multi-semester simulation with carry-over mechanics for engagement, GPA, coping, dropout phases
  • GPA feedback loop anchoring cost-benefit, non-fit perception, and competence beliefs

Calibration & Validation

  • Sobol sensitivity analysis (68 parameters) + NSGA-II multi-objective optimization with Pareto front
  • 5-level validation suite: 21 statistical tests (distributions, correlations, temporal coherence, privacy, backstory)
  • Adaptive parameter bounds adjusting calibration space to demographic changes

Configuration & Output

  • InstitutionalConfig (5 quality params), GradingConfig (Beta/Normal/Uniform, dual-hurdle, relative grading), EngineConfig (70 constants), PipelineConfig (JSON serialization)
  • OULAD-compatible 7-table CSV export, optional LLM-enriched backstories, benchmark reports
  • Interactive dashboard (Shiny for Python + Plotly): dark-themed UI with configuration presets, reactive distribution validation, 4 summary cards and charts, JSON config import/export

Use cases: dropout prediction, intervention simulation, privacy-safe benchmarking, EDM research in GDPR/KVKK-constrained settings.
